texto: We have been cut off from our souls in the West, and because romantic love has become our religion, we think we can find fulfillment through this extraordinary and powerful force that draws us into an illusion of permanence. Passion makes us feel alive, makes us sing, makes us feel in touch with something powerful and wonderful, just as it would if we followed this meaning in life in a more spiritual practice. In the West it is often through such relationships, through another human being, that we search desperately for something, not knowing it is to be found within ourselves.
explicacao: Citação de Sarah Bartlett sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “We have been cut off from our souls in the West, and because romantic love has become our religio…” articula reflexao, love, meaning em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como meaning e soul.
